feed.title: Amir Razmjou
feed.subtitle: Writing on systems, networks, and software engineering.

entry.title: Gerdu: A Multi-Protocol, Raft-Backed Key-Value Store in Go
entry.summary: Notes on building Gerdu, an in-memory key-value store in Go: one small cache interface behind LRU/LFU/weak implementations, four wire protocols at once, and Raft replication where the cache itself is the state machine.

entry.title: Building Sentry: A Secure Job Runner with cgroups and mTLS
entry.summary: Notes on building Sentry, a small Go service that runs Linux processes under hard CPU, memory, and I/O limits with cgroups v2, streams their logs over gRPC, and authenticates everyone with mutual TLS.

entry.title: Building a Search Engine in Rust: Crawl, Index, Rank
entry.summary: Building RSeek, a web crawler and full-text search engine in Rust: a Semaphore-bounded Tokio crawler, an inverted index, and BM25 ranking that beats naive keyword counting.

entry.title: Writing a Small S3 Proxy in Rust
entry.summary: Notes on building a thin, S3-compatible proxy in Rust with axum and the AWS SDK: routing requests to the right backend by bucket name, API-key auth with per-user roles and allow-lists, and a sliding-window rate limiter.

entry.title: PathForge: Writing a BGP-4 Daemon from Scratch in Rust
entry.summary: Notes on building PathForge, a from-scratch BGP-4 daemon in Rust: wire framing you can fuzz, the session state machine, one Tokio task per peer, and the best-path decision process expressed as a sort comparator.

entry.title: Snake on a Cube: 3D Snake in Rust and WebAssembly
entry.summary: snake3d-rs: Snake crawling across all six faces of a cube, compiled to WebAssembly with three-d. The whole game lives in the seams where one face's coordinates meet another's.

entry.title: Reimplementing S3 from Scratch: An Object Store in Rust
entry.summary: Building R3, an S3-compatible object store in Rust: filesystem-backed objects, multipart upload assembly, and versioning with delete markers — where the real difficulty of S3 turns out to be the metadata, not the bytes.

entry.title: NetChaos: Declarative Network Chaos with eBPF and Kubernetes
entry.summary: NetChaos, a Kubernetes-native network fault injection framework: describe failures as CRDs, drive them with an operator reconcile loop, and deliver packet drops at kernel speed with eBPF.

entry.title: How a Local IP Address Broke IPv6 Tunnels in the Linux Kernel
entry.summary: The story of a one-line Linux kernel fix: while routing one app's traffic by UID into a FOU/SIT tunnel, encapsulated IPv6 to a local peer silently vanished — an RTN_LOCAL route the transmit path refused to allow.

entry.title: OpenStack REST API Direct Call
entry.summary: Some OpenStack wrapper libraries like OpenStack4j or Cloud4j does not cover all OpenStack REST API. I tried to explain a simple and general approach for OpenStack developers to perform the same common OpenStack CLI tools tasks in Java.

entry.title: Data-Mining PCAP files using Weka and Python DPKT library
entry.summary: Using Weka, Python DPKT to find malicious traffic flows in FTP server control flows.

entry.title: GSoC 2015 RouteFlow Proposal
entry.summary: Here it is my Google Summer of Code proposal for unified end-to-end tests of RouteFlow project for 2015

entry.title: Playing With K-nearest Neighbors Algorithm
entry.summary: This program is an inefficient (in terms of computing and memory complexity) yet simple demonstration to implement k-nearest neighbors unsupervised classification algorithm.

entry.title: Why does functional programming matter?
entry.summary: Like any other missing feature in any programming language imperative programming languages address these problems by making more libraries with complex interfaces. Likewise any built-in language supported feature can result in code clarity, elegance and maintainability and leave the responsibility of optimization and other concerns to programming language in order to have programmers focus on their problems.

entry.title: What Is Computer Security?
entry.summary: This semester, Dr. Heather Crawford asked us to wirte a review about the paper titled "What Is Computer Security?" written by MattBishop.

entry.title: Collecting Data Using Windows Azure Part 2
entry.summary: How to obtain beautifull network graphs by collecting twitter data

entry.title: Spanning Tree
entry.summary: Just about everything you'll need to style in the theme: headings, paragraphs, blockquotes, tables, code blocks, and more.

entry.title: Hello Complex Networks!!!
entry.summary: I just wanted to refresh my JavaScript programming skills along with exercising my homeworks. I searched few Network Visualization JS libraries in the net I came up with VisJS. It’s pretty easy to create networks in VisJS.

entry.title: Collecting Data using Windows Azure Part 1
entry.summary: I decided to develop an Azure WebJob to collect my twitter data into MongoDB, using LinqToTwitter library along with MongoDB made this job pretty easy.

entry.title: QoS optimization for real-time services in FreeBSD
entry.summary: VSAT systems generally connect a large number of geographically dispersed sites to a central location. one of my customers offers VSAT service to different types of business including banking, telecomm.
